error.signature: Slack API returns 429 Too Many Requests on one method while other methods work fine

verdict.summary: Slack rate limits are per-method and per-workspace, not global. chat.postMessage has ~1 req/sec per channel. conversations.list has different limits. Hitting the limit on one method doesn't affect others. The Retry-After header tells you exactly how long to wait.

dead_ends.action: Implement a single global rate limiter for all Slack API calls
dead_ends.why_fails: Rate limits are per-method. A global limiter is either too restrictive (slows unaffected methods) or too loose (still hits per-method limits).
Immediate retry will also be rate-limited. Always respect the Retry-After header value.

workarounds.action: Implement per-method rate limiting using Slack's published tier limits
workarounds.how: Tier 1: 1/min, Tier 2: 20/min, Tier 3: 50/min, Tier 4: 100/min. chat.postMessage is Tier 2-3 depending on context.

workarounds.action: Always respect the Retry-After header on 429 responses
Use Slack's Web API with built-in rate limit handling (slack_sdk)
workarounds.how: from slack_sdk import WebClient; client = WebClient(token=token)  # automatically handles rate limits with retries
